title: Picea numidica
intro: Picea numidica, also known as Algerian fir, is a coniferous tree species native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Picea numidica include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Picea numidica include essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Picea numidica is classified as a species of evergreen coniferous tree in the family Pinaceae. It belongs to the genus Picea and is native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. Picea numidica is also known as Algerian spruce. Within the Pinaceae family, Picea numidica is closely related to other species such as Picea pungens and Picea omorika. Its classification is part of the broader taxonomy of coniferous trees in the Pinaceae family.
